title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 3, 2026 - Market Closed, Reviewing Friday's Performance

items.headline: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Hit New Records on Friday, Dow Slips as Q1 Earnings Season Kicks Off Strong
items.summary: U.S. equity markets closed a robust week on Friday, May 1, 2026, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite reaching new all-time highs. Strong first-quarter earnings reports, particularly from major technology companies, largely fueled the gains. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, however, saw a slight decline. Easing oil prices also contributed to a generally positive market sentiment. The upcoming week will bring a fresh wave of earnings and key economic data, including the monthly jobs report. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Apple Surges on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Apple's stock ($AAPL) jumped over 3% on Friday after reporting robust quarterly earnings and providing optimistic guidance for the period ahead.

items.headline: Atlassian Corp. Leads Gainers
items.summary: Atlassian Corp. ($TEAM) was among the top performers on Friday, seeing a significant increase following market activity.

items.headline: Salesforce Posts Gains
items.summary: Salesforce ($CRM) was a notable gainer within the Dow Jones Industrial Average on Friday, contributing positively to the index.

items.headline: Roblox Tumbles on Weak Outlook
items.summary: Roblox ($RBLX) stock tumbled 24% in premarket trading on Friday after the online game platform slashed its full-year 2026 bookings guidance, signaling a weaker outlook.

items.headline: Meta Drops Amid AI Spending Concerns
items.summary: Meta Platforms ($META) experienced a significant weekly decline of 9.8%, despite reporting better-than-expected earnings, as investor concerns grew over increased capital expenditure estimates for AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Robinhood Markets Disappoints
items.summary: Robinhood Markets ($HOOD) saw a weekly decline of 13.04%, closing at $73.66, after its quarterly earnings release failed to impress the market.

items.headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Season Shows Robust Growth
items.summary: The first-quarter 2026 earnings season is in full swing, with the S&P 500's blended earnings growth rate for the quarter currently at 27.1% year-over-year. A significant 84% of S&P 500 companies have reported actual EPS above estimates, surpassing historical averages.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Interest Rates Steady
items.summary: The Federal Reserve Board maintained interest rates in the 3.50% – 3.75% range, as widely anticipated. Recent economic data showed March PCE inflation rising to 3.5% and core PCE to 3.2%.

items.headline: First Quarter GDP Growth at 2.0%
items.summary: The preliminary first-quarter GDP reading indicated an annual growth rate of 2.0%, slightly below analysts' expectations but remaining modestly positive.

items.headline: Iran Peace Proposal Cools Oil Prices
items.summary: Oil prices experienced a drop, with U.S. crude falling 3% and Brent crude down 2%, following news of Iran submitting a new peace proposal via Pakistan. This raised hopes for a potential U.S.-Iran settlement and the eventual resumption of oil exports.

items.headline: Major Earnings Ahead: Pfizer, AMD, Disney and More
items.summary: The week of May 4, 2026, is set to be another busy one for earnings, with 128 S&P 500 companies scheduled to report. Notable companies include Pfizer ($PFE), Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD), Emerson Electric ($EMR), Walt Disney ($DIS), McDonald's ($MCD), and Zoetis ($ZTS).

items.headline: Big Tech Ramps Up AI Spending, Driving Sector Activity
items.summary: Major technology companies are significantly increasing their borrowing to fund massive AI spending, with total planned spending reaching approximately $725 billion this year. This trend is a major driver of activity within the tech sector.
